message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: My feature: add error_threshold parameter to Image.quantize(). I need to edit Image.py (quantize method) and its tests. If your feature also touches Image.py, let's agree on a split (e.g. different methods/sections) or I can go first and you rebase, whichever is easier.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Adding sort_palette param to Image.quantize() for custom palette sorting after quantization. I need to touch Image.py (quantize method) and its test file. Let me know your feature/files so we can keep edits disjoint.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Our features both live in quantize() so full file-disjointness isn't possible. Since we each submit independent patch.txt (no real merge happens here), let's just avoid stepping on each other conceptually: I'll add my new sort_palette param as an additional kwarg at the end of the quantize() signature (after existing params, with its own default so it's fully backward compatible), and only add new logic in a small block near the end of the method body. I'll append my new test cases at the end of test_image_quantize.py as new functions, not touching existing tests. Please do the same for error_threshold (append param at end of signature, isolated logic block, new test functions appended at end). This keeps our diffs conceptually orthogonal even though same file. Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: We both need Image.py + test file since both features extend quantize(). Since we can't fully separate files, let's separate by section to minimize collision: (1) signature - I'll add my new param 'error_threshold' as the LAST parameter after dither, please add your 'sort_palette' param also after dither but after mine (order: colors, method, kmeans, palette, dither, error_threshold, sort_palette) so we each append to the end rather than editing the same spot; (2) body - my logic (looping/checking MSE) lives in a self-contained block BEFORE the final 'return im' in the non-palette branch, computing/checking error and possibly re-quantizing with fewer colors; your sort_palette logic should be a separate self-contained block AFTER quantization produces 'im'/'new_im' (e.g. reordering im.palette) so it doesn't need to touch my loop internals. Docstring: I'll add my :param error_threshold: entry right after :param dither:, please add your :param sort_palette: entry after mine. This keeps our diffs in distinct hunks. Agreed?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Agreed: signature order colors, method, kmeans, palette, dither, error_threshold, sort_palette. My sort_palette block goes after quantization produces im/new_im (reordering palette), after your error_threshold block. Docstring: my :param sort_palette: entry goes after your :param error_threshold: entry. I'll append my new test functions at the end of test_image_quantize.py. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Accepted. I'll append error_threshold=None as a new kwarg at the end of quantize()'s signature (after dither), add an isolated logic block for it near the end of the method body, add its own :param error_threshold: docstring entry at the end of the docstring, and append new test functions at the end of test_image_quantize.py without touching existing tests/lines. Will send DONE when finished.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Finished error_threshold implementation: signature is quantize(self, colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, error_threshold=None) - append your sort_palette param right after error_threshold. Docstring :param error_threshold: is the last entry before :returns:, add your :param sort_palette: after mine. Logic block is a self-contained 'if error_threshold is not None:' block right after the existing im.palette assignment (around line 1127), before 'return im' - place your sort_palette logic after mine (still before return im). New tests appended at end of test_image_quantize.py as 3 new functions, existing tests untouched. All tests pass locally. Good luck with sort_palette!

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Finished sort_palette feature: added sort_palette param (after dither) to quantize(), a new Image._sort_palette_colors() helper that reorders the palette by luminance or RGB value and remaps pixel indices via point() + putpalette() (needed putpalette specifically, not direct .palette assignment, so the underlying C image data actually gets the new palette - plain attribute assignment only updates the Python-side mirror). Added 3 new test functions appended at the end of test_image_quantize.py. All existing + new tests pass, black-formatted. Patch written to patch.txt.
